title: Audit a surface for cross-endpoint consistency
description: A procedure to review an existing API for divergent naming, shapes, and error handling and to converge it.
body: Use when an API has grown organically and feels inconsistent. Do not fix one endpoint in isolation. Steps: inventory resources, field names, casing, error shapes, and paging across endpoints; identify the same concept modeled differently; define the target convention; converge additively and deprecate the outliers. Validation signals include a single predictable shape per concept and a lint pass against the style guide. Risk boundary: changing shapes can break clients, so converge through additive aliases and deprecation rather than in-place renames.
